evidence: We have begun and may continue to use artificial intelligence and machine learning (collectively, AI) tools in our operations. We use AI in assessing marketing decisions and operating our stores.
We leverage AI-enabled security tools to assist in identifying anomalous activity, to enhance threat detection, and in our incident response processes.
Uncertainty around the safety and security of new and emerging AI applications may require additional investment in the development of proprietary datasets, machine learning models and systems
We use AI in assessing marketing decisions and operating our stores.
We leverage AI-enabled security tools to assist in identifying anomalous activity, to enhance threat detection, and in our incident response processes.
We also consider risks associated with AI initiatives within our cybersecurity risk management program and apply appropriate controls to help protect AI related systems and information.

AI tools may introduce errors or inadequacies that are not easily detectable, including deficiencies, inaccuracies, or biases in the data used for AI training, or in the content, analyses, or recommendations generated by AI applications.
if our peers use AI tools to optimize operations and we fail to utilize AI tools in a comparable manner, we may be competitively disadvantaged.

Cybersecurity threat actors may also utilize AI tools to automate and enhance cybersecurity attacks against us and could lead to data breaches, loss of confidential or sensitive information, and financial or reputational harm.

limitations: No quantified AI-attributable dollar amounts, headcount, or capex figures appear in the excerpts; investment intensity capped at 3.

AI use cases (marketing decisions, store operations, security tools) are named and described as deployed but no quantified business results (%, $, time saved) are provided, limiting concreteness to 4.

Risk disclosure is company-specific with named mechanisms (competitive disadvantage, bias/inaccuracy, cybersecurity attack amplification, regulatory compliance costs) but no quantified risk exposure or formal named oversight body beyond general board quarterly briefings, limiting depth to 4.
Excerpts are pre-filtered; additional AI investment or risk detail may exist elsewhere in the filing.
